headline: With G7 leaders, Trump seeks to ease stress on oil markets
description: The group of Western powers, which have remained cautious about the war in Iran waged by Israel and the US, met on Wednesday to try to minimize the conflict's impact on the global economy.

headline: 'The Islamism of the Iranian regime is now reduced to a rather worn veneer'
description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader, in a regime that calls itself an Islamic Republic, already signals a form of defeat in the ongoing war waged by the US and Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: Certain cancers may be less common among vegetarians
description: A study based on 15 years of monitoring several cohorts in four countries found that individuals who abstain from eating meat have a lower risk of developing multiple myeloma, pancreatic, breast, prostate and kidney cancer.

headline: An exhibition where art confronts death
description: The Pinault Collection's new exhibition at the Bourse de Commerce, 'Clair-obscur,' features some 100 works by 27 artists that explore the theme of death.

headline: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
description: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
description: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

headline: Maersk CEO: 'In Asia and the Middle East, our fuel supply points risk running dry'
description: The head of the Danish maritime transport company tells Le Monde how the war in the Middle East has forced the world's second-largest shipping line to deploy new sea routes. Ten of the company's ships are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf.

headline: In southern Beirut: 'Dahiyeh is destroyed, but that changes nothing for us. We support Hezbollah.'
description: On the outskirts of the deserted Shiite neighborhoods in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, militants and a few remaining residents watch helplessly as Israeli strikes destroy entire areas, day after day.
